headline: Penang Hill Development Adds 2,800 Vehicle Trips Daily
context: Penang Island City Council data shows development approvals on Penang Hill's slopes generated an estimated 2,800 additional daily vehicle trips in 2024, worsening landslide risk.

cards.big: Penang Hill has 14 active construction sites above 250 metres elevation.
cards.sub: The 2017 Penang Structure Plan capped hilltop development. 11 of the 14 projects were approved after the cap.

cards.big: Slope failures on Penang Hill increased from 8 in 2019 to 31 in 2024.
cards.sub: JKR classified 4 slopes as 'immediate danger' but no evacuation orders were issued.

cards.big: Property values on Penang Hill average RM1,200 per sq ft.
cards.sub: Development yields are 3x flatland rates — creating enormous financial pressure to approve hillside projects.

cards.big: The Penang Hills Special Area Plan has been pending approval since 2019.
cards.sub: 6 years of delay means ongoing approvals proceed under older, less restrictive guidelines.

cards.big: A special plan that has been pending for 6 years is not pending. It is being avoided.
Hill development moratoriums work when enforced. Penang needs to approve its own plan and stop issuing exemptions to a cap that exists on paper.
